reviews.name: Mohammed Abdul Wasi
reviews.text:
My experience with Dr. Gangadhar has been absolutely wonderful. His skills in rehabilitation and physiotherapy are truly excellent. I was amazed at how effective his treatment was — I recovered completely within just 15 days!

Dr. Gangadhar’s approach is highly professional, focused, and result-oriented. He carefully understands the issue and provides the right exercises and therapy that bring quick and lasting relief.

I would highly recommend Gangadhar Rehabilitation Centre to anyone looking for a good physiotherapy centre in Hyderabad, especially for sports-related injuries. The treatment and care here are simply fabulous!

Not only the doctor, but each and every staff member of this hospital is humble, down-to-earth, generous, and kind. I will keep praising this hospital. It is truly a marvellous hospital for the middle class.
May God bless them with immense success in the overall development of this hospital and guide them to continue showing mercy towards the needy.

reviews.name: Revanth reddy
reviews.text:
This hospital is a complete scam. We booked an appointment with Dr. Nishith, but he was never available (twice). Each time, they redirected us to Dr. Gopal. Instead of proper treatment, they kept pushing unnecessary tests — first a CT scan, then expensive blood tests at their own center.

Finally, they suggested laparoscopy. Even with insurance coverage, they gave an inflated estimate of ₹1,30,000, while other reputed hospitals charge around ₹80,000. And shockingly, they admitted there is no guarantee it will work — if it doesn’t, they would again push for another surgery.

This hospital is only interested in looting patients through tests and inflated bills. Absolutely unethical, unprofessional, and money-minded. Do yourself a favor and avoid this place.

Don't say me to do something better in life. You people are simply eating customer money. Already this hospital has been reported and came in news as well. I've attached photo as well
reviews.responseFromOwnerText:
Dear Revanth Reddy,
Never ever anything of this sort has happened. We kindly ask you to furnish your/patient details. In case, if you fail to provide exact details, we condemn such baseless feedback or reviews and treat this as spam and also a small advice - PLEASE FIND SOMETHING BETTER TO DO IN LIFE.
Thanks & Regards
Hyderabad Multispeciality Hospital
7680883666

reviews.name: Revanth Reddy
reviews.text:
This hospital is nothing but a scam. We booked an appointment with Dr. Nishith, but he was never available — not once, but twice. Instead, they kept redirecting us to Dr. Gopal. From there it was only about money-making: first forcing us into an unnecessary CT scan, then again pushing us to do costly blood tests at their own center.

Even after all this, they gave no clear conclusion or treatment plan. Total waste of time, money, and peace of mind. They are running a business in the name of healthcare, with zero ethics. .Don’t fall into their trap.

Don't say me to do something better in life. You people are simply eating customer money. Already this hospital has been reported and came in news as well. I've attached photo as well

I wanted to personally thank the entire team for their extraordinary care and treatment provided in the due course of the stay at hospital.

reviews.name: Sridhar radhika
reviews.text:
I had a wonderful experience at Hyderabad Multispeciality Hospital. Dr. Pradeep Reddy and his excellent team truly stand out—they are among the best I've seen in recent times. Their care, dedication, and motivation towards patients are remarkable. The hospital provides top-notch physiotherapy services, arguably the best in the city. Dr. Pradeep Reddy is not just a doctor; he is truly a God’s angel in the way he treats and supports his patients. I highly recommend this hospital for anyone seeking quality healthcare with compassion.
Thank you Doctor and Staff...

reviews.name: Amtul Aziz Arshia
reviews.text:
My mom ( Noor Jahan) recently underwent knee replacement surgery at this hospital, performed by Dr. Pradeep and his excellent team. From the initial consultation to the surgery and recovery, everything was handled with professionalism and care. The surgery went incredibly well, and my mom is now on her way to a smooth recovery, thanks to the expertise and dedication of the doctors.

A huge thank you to the nurses, physiotherapy team, and support staff who were always kind, attentive, and ready to help at any moment. Their positive attitude and encouragement made a significant difference in my mom’s recovery process. The physiotherapy sessions were well-planned and effective, giving her the confidence to regain her mobility.

The hospital services were top-notch, with cleanliness, efficiency, and patient care being a clear priority. I’m deeply grateful for the kindness and support shown to us during this time. Highly recommend this hospital for anyone seeking exceptional medical care!

We definitely recommend HMH hospital to our friends and relatives at any of their ailments.  Special thanks to Pavani mam and Pradeep sir for their dedication towards patients.

reviews.name: sivaram kumar
reviews.text:
Our family doctor recommended Hyderabad Multispeciality Hospital as they are highly regarded for their expertise in urology treatments. I visited the hospital on October 14, 2024, and paid a consultation fee of Rs. 650. As a first step, a nurse checked my weight, height, and blood pressure and asked if I had any known conditions like diabetes or hypertension. After these initial checks, I was referred to an assistant doctor, who discussed my concerns and prescribed some tests, including an overall blood profile and an ultrasound.

I immediately provided a blood sample and then went to the ultrasound center within the hospital. By the time I arrived, it was 3 PM, and I had to wait 1.5 hours for the scan as the technician was on a lunch break. While the lab was basic, it was functional, and the doctor there diagnosed me with an inguinal right hernia. With the reports in hand, I met with the main doctor, Dr. Nitish Reddy, a urologist. After reviewing my reports and conducting a physical exam, he confirmed that surgery would be necessary and recommended an open procedure on the affected groin area. Although he is a urologist, he does not perform surgeries himself, so he consulted Dr. Kaushik Reddy, a general surgeon. Dr. Nitish discussed the case with Dr. Kaushik in my presence, and they both recommended open surgery instead of laparoscopic, which I agreed to. They suggested a few potential dates, and I was instructed to check in a half day before the surgery.

Following the consultation, Dr. Nitish referred me for an ECG and a pre-surgery checkup with Dr. Rahul, the anesthesiologist, who reviewed my ECG and X-ray reports and approved the surgery. Dr. Rahul was very friendly and provided clear instructions on pre- and post-surgery care. I left the hospital feeling well-informed and called them on October 13, 2024, as agreed, to confirm my admission.

I arrived at the hospital at 10:00 PM with my brother, as it was required to have someone with me. After completing the payment, I was assigned a clean room on the 3rd floor, where the staff were kind and supportive. Early the next morning, at around 6:00 AM, an attendant helped me prepare for surgery. I took a shower, changed clothes, and the nurse administered an IV with antibiotics, ensuring I was comfortable throughout.

At 6:45 AM, I was taken to the operating room, where Dr. Rahul explained the anesthesia procedure. He administered spinal anesthesia, ensuring I wouldn’t feel anything below my abdomen. Dr. Kaushik, assisted by Dr. Lakshmi, began the surgery at 7:00 AM. Dr. Rahul even offered to play some devotional music, which made the experience calming. The procedure went smoothly, and by 8:00 AM, the surgery was completed successfully. Dr. Kaushik prescribed some post-operative care instructions, and I was moved to the ICU, where I remained until 4:00 PM, receiving medication and pain relief through the IV.

Around 12:30 PM, Dr. Nitish visited, checked on my progress, and reassured me that the surgery had gone well. The ICU staff were attentive and took great care of me. By 4:30 PM, I was moved back to my room.

The following morning, I felt some relief from the pain, had some breakfast, and managed to take a few steps with the help of the attendants. Although I experienced pain throughout the night, the staff continued to care for me diligently. By Monday morning, I was feeling much better, and Dr. Nitish visited again to assess my condition, change my dressing, and discharge me with instructions to return on October 25 for a follow-up.
This was my first surgery, and the experience at HMS was very positive. The staff were caring, experienced, and avoided unnecessary tests, which I appreciated. The surgery cost was also reasonable compared to other hospitals like Apollo. I would wholeheartedly recommend HMS for similar procedures. I am extremely satisfied with the staff’s support and Dr. Nitish’s expertise. It’s now the third week since my surgery, and I am recovering well with no post-surgery complications.

Thank you,
Sivaram
reviews.responseFromOwnerText:
Thanks for Rating us 5 Stars. Happy to serve our patients and wishing you good health. Nevertheless, we try improving our services every day. 
Thanks & Regards, 
Hyderabad MultiSpeciality Hospital Team.

reviews.name: Shaik Mohammed
reviews.text:
Hi Team! Would want to share this disheartened experience.

My first visit was on Tue ( 8th July'24 ) for my mom's consultation. We were advised to take Xray and we've followed it. Post getting xray film. We've waited exactly 50 mins to get our number to consult doctor. There were only 1 patient who was in the cabin and took 50 mins... I mean, does that take 50 mins for just consultation???

Today, 12th July'24. I have come for the review cause there was no improvement in the treatment which was given. It's 12:30 pm and doctor isn't available here. When asked, the receptionist is saying orthopaedic doctor will come in 10 mins.

We've already waited for 30 mins and still hoping for doctor to come....

Please value and respect the customers time and their importance. We do have other works to take care of than sitting in the hospital and waiting for the doctor to come...

reviews.text: I had to wait for 2-3 hours to get my issue checked with the doctor and the receptionist's behaviour was so rude and unprofessional. reviews.name: Mohd Saif
reviews.text:
Im a football player  Since last 3 months Im having Serious Shoulder pain and  im talking Medication but there is no result Then i consulted Dr Gangadhar Physiotherapist and he started Ultrasound Therapy & Dry Needling Therapy After He advised Scapular Stretching Exercises Now im completely Fine And im going back to my work Thanks to Dr Gangadhar sir Best Physiotherapist 🥼 Doctor in Hyderabad Multispecialty Hospital
Malakpet.

reviews.name: Swathi J
reviews.text: Overall experience with the hospital is very good. Excellent doctors especially Dr. G Vijay Kumar Reddy garu (Ortho) and Dr. R Suneel garu (Ortho). The type of responses from the doctors and staff are very helpful. We stepped out of hospital with a smile and could see immediate improvement post surgery. Ms. Pavani is very helpful and arranged everything in an orderly way with regards to hospitalization formalities. The billing process is transparent. An excellent hospital with ethical standards and fair practices. We would surely recommend the hospital.

reviews.name: Prashanthi Reddy
reviews.text: The best hospital in hyderabad with best doctors, Dr Nishith Reddy and Dr vishnu vardhan Reddy are great doctors they treated my mother with care and humanity, and Dr Ramesh is also too good for orthopedic, and now Hyd kidney center is now Hyderabad multi specialty hospital for all treatments in very low cost, recently in past few month I have visited other hospitals which are very commercial but hyd multi specialty hospital is not commercial its collecting reasonable fee from patients and giving great service to patients, and anesthesia doctor Dr Rahul,  he is very caring, soft and he asked for very minor issues that we have, which I didn't find in other hospitals, I'm very grateful to entire team.

outreach.subject: Recovering ₹87.5 lakhs/month for HKC Hospital | Hyderabad Kidney Centre | Multi-Speciality Hospital | Since 1999
outreach.body:
Dear To be found via LinkedIn,

I hope this email finds you well.

I came across HKC Hospital | Hyderabad Kidney Centre | Multi-Speciality Hospital | Since 1999 in Hyderabad, Telangana and noticed you're processing approximately 1000 insurance claims monthly.

Based on industry benchmarks, you're likely losing ₹87.5 lakhs every month due to:
• 30-40% claim rejection rate
• Manual processing delays (45-90 days)
• Data entry errors causing rejections

**We've built an AI system specifically for Indian hospitals that:**
✅ Validates claims BEFORE submission (reduces rejections to <10%)
✅ Auto-fills insurance forms in seconds (zero errors)
✅ Tracks and recovers rejected claims automatically

**The Numbers:**
• Your estimated monthly loss: ₹87.5 lakhs
• Recoverable with our system: ₹61.2 lakhs/month
• Our cost: ₹35,000/month
• Your ROI: 175.0x return
• Payback: < 1 month

**Free Audit Offer:**
Let me analyze your last 100 claims and show you:
1. Exact rejection patterns
2. Exact ₹ amount being lost
3. Exact recovery potential

Takes 2 days. No cost. No commitment.

Would you be open to a 15-minute call this week?

Best regards,
[Your Name]
[Your Company]
[Your Phone/WhatsApp]

P.S. We've already helped hospitals in Hyderabad and Bangalore recover ₹5-12 lakhs/month. Happy to share case studies.
